  "clarification_question": "Which focus do you want for this section: (A) sentence-level polish and clarity only, (B) substantive content review to ensure logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment, or (C) higher-level reframing/structure and positioning vs prior work?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Conceptual reframing may be needed to align the section’s core thesis and contribution with what is actually established.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the argument from theoretical claims to conclusions is logically sound is critical for this section.",
    "R1.3": "Interpretations and claims should be checked against the reported metrics and results for alignment.",
    "R1.6": "Polishing sentence-level clarity and concision will improve readability and professional tone.",
    "RX.1": "Given the ambiguous request, any assistance should explicitly note uncertainties and limits of inference.",
    "RX.2": "The section makes strong claims, so guidance must avoid introducing or reinforcing overstatements.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay strictly within the scope of improving this section rather than expanding its experimental or methodological content.",
    "RX.4": "Calibration claims can affect downstream agent safety, so recommendations should avoid encouraging unsafe or misleading interpretations.",
    "RX.5": "Suggestions should be traceable to specific sentences and cited sources to maintain provenance and accountability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus do you want for this section: (A) sentence-level polish and clarity only, (B) substantive content review to ensure logical consistency and evidence-claim alignment, or (C) higher-level reframing/structure and positioning vs prior work?

rationale.R1.1: Conceptual reframing may be needed to align the section’s core thesis and contribution with what is actually established.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the argument from theoretical claims to conclusions is logically sound is critical for this section.
rationale.R1.3: Interpretations and claims should be checked against the reported metrics and results for alignment.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level clarity and concision will improve readability and professional tone.
rationale.RX.1: Given the ambiguous request, any assistance should explicitly note uncertainties and limits of inference.
rationale.RX.2: The section makes strong claims, so guidance must avoid introducing or reinforcing overstatements.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay strictly within the scope of improving this section rather than expanding its experimental or methodological content.
rationale.RX.4: Calibration claims can affect downstream agent safety, so recommendations should avoid encouraging unsafe or misleading interpretations.
rationale.RX.5: Suggestions should be traceable to specific sentences and cited sources to maintain provenance and accountability.
